questions.question: Why do meristematic cells lack vacuoles?
questions.answer: Because they need dense cytoplasm for rapid cell division.

questions.question: Why do plants have more supportive tissues than animals?
questions.answer: Because plants are stationary and must remain upright.

questions.question: Why are sclerenchyma cells dead?
questions.answer: Their thick lignified walls leave no internal space for living components.

questions.question: What is lignin?
questions.answer: Lignin is a hard substance that strengthens cell walls in sclerenchyma.

questions.question: What are vascular bundles?
questions.answer: Vascular bundles are groups of xylem and phloem tissues.

questions.question: What is the function of sieve tubes?
questions.answer: They transport food in plants.

questions.question: What are guard cells?
questions.answer: Guard cells are specialized cells that control opening and closing of stomata.

questions.question: What is cuticle in plants?
questions.answer: Cuticle is a waxy protective layer on the epidermis that reduces water loss.

questions.question: What is suberin?
questions.answer: Suberin is a substance in cork cells that makes them waterproof.

questions.question: What is stratified squamous epithelium?
questions.answer: It is layered epithelial tissue that protects body surfaces from wear and tear.

questions.question: What is glandular epithelium?
questions.answer: Glandular epithelium is epithelial tissue specialized for secretion.

questions.question: Why is bone considered a connective tissue?
questions.answer: Because it supports and connects different body parts.

questions.question: Why are ligaments elastic?
questions.answer: To allow movement while connecting bones at joints.

questions.question: Why are tendons less flexible than ligaments?
questions.answer: Because tendons mainly provide strong attachment between muscles and bones.

questions.question: What is hyaline cartilage?
questions.answer: Hyaline cartilage is a smooth cartilage that reduces friction at joints.

questions.question: Why is blood considered a connective tissue?
questions.answer: Because it connects body parts by transporting substances.

questions.question: What are muscle fibres?
questions.answer: Muscle fibres are elongated cells that form muscular tissue.

questions.question: Why are striated muscles called voluntary muscles?
questions.answer: Because they work under conscious control.

questions.question: Why are smooth muscles called involuntary muscles?
questions.answer: Because their movements are not controlled consciously.

questions.question: Why are cardiac muscles unique?
questions.answer: Because they are branched and work continuously throughout life.

questions.question: What is the function of dendrites?
questions.answer: Dendrites receive nerve impulses from other neurons.

questions.question: What is the function of axon?
questions.answer: Axon carries nerve impulses away from the cell body.

questions.question: What forms a nerve?
questions.answer: Many nerve fibres bundled together form a nerve.

questions.question: Why is nervous tissue essential for coordination?
questions.answer: Because it transmits signals between body parts quickly.

questions.question: Why is the combination of muscle and nerve tissue important?
questions.answer: It allows animals to respond quickly to stimuli.
